photographer: Dr. Thomas G. Barnes, U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service
creditLine: Dr. Thomas G. Barnes, U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service
caption: Close-up of a black-eyed Susan flower and bud.
publicDomainBasis: Wikimedia Commons file page states the work was released into the public domain by Dr. Thomas G. Barnes, U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service, for worldwide reuse.
reviewNotes: Chosen as the live bundled image because the flower and bud are centered and substantially better for plant identification than the earlier garden-scene image.

caption: Black-eyed Susan planting in a USDA ARS pollinator garden.
publicDomainBasis: Hosted on an official USDA ARS image page and reviewed under USDA policy stating most USDA website materials are public domain unless otherwise noted.
reviewNotes: Legally usable backup candidate, but not preferred for the live detail page because the composition includes a person and does not center the plant clearly enough for identification.

caption: Black-eyed Susan blooming in the Alabama ES garden.
publicDomainBasis: USFWS media page explicitly labels the asset as Public Domain.
reviewNotes: Preferred government-source candidate. Safe to bundle locally after manual download and final metadata check.
